english: all one's life; throughout one's life

etymology.description: 一(一) (yī, "one") + 辈(輩) (bèi, "generation") + 子(子) (zi, "noun suffix")
etymology.interpretation: The term 一輩子 (yíbèizi) literally translates to 'one generation', with the addition of the suffix 子(子) (zi, "noun suffix") to denote a duration of time encompassing one's entire lifetime, emphasizing continuity and entirety.

examples.chinese: 我想一辈子守护你(我想一輩子守護你)。
examples.english: I want to protect you all my life.

examples.chinese: 他一辈子都在追求真理(他一輩子都在追求真理)。
examples.pinyin: Tā yíbèizi dōu zài zhuīqiú zhēnlǐ.
examples.english: He has been pursuing the truth all his life.

examples.chinese: 一辈子生活在这个小镇上(一輩子生活在這個小鎮上)，他已经和这里融为一体(他已經和這裡融為一體)。
examples.pinyin: Yíbèizi shēnghuó zài zhège xiǎo zhèn shàng, tā yǐjīng hé zhèlǐ róng wéi yī tǐ.
examples.english: Having lived in this small town all his life, he has become one with it.
